intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant Echinella pan. It is possible that the plant you are referring to is actually Echinacea, a flowering plant in the daisy family. 

If we assume the plant you are referring to is actually Echinacea, here is a paragraph about it:

Echinacea is a genus of flowering plants that are commonly known as coneflowers, which are native to North America. Echinacea has been traditionally used to boost the immune system and to treat various health conditions, including colds, flu, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea include alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Echinacea include teas, tinctures, capsules, and extracts, which are often used to prevent illness and to support immune system function.
